macro_segments.title: The Debate Between Rabba and Rav Yosef on Assessment (Shuma) of Silk for Kiddushin

micro_segments.title: Introduction: The Two Versions of the Debate
Rav Yosef's Position: Shaveh Kessef Must Functionally Be Like Kessef (Kayatz Principle)
Rav Yosef's Source: The Eved Ivri and the Braitta on Tevuah and Kelim
Rabba's Response: Shaveh Kessef Functions on Its Own Terms, Not as Quasi-Money
The Pidyon Ha-Ben Debate: Fixed Value vs. Subjective Valuation
Tosafot's Distinction: Shiraim (Silk) Don't Require Assessment; Precious Stones Do

macro_segments.title: The Case of Partial Money: Maneh Stated, Dinar Given, and the Concept of Tenai

micro_segments.title: Reisha: Maneh Stam (Unstated Amount) and Counting Out Money
Seifa: Maneh Zu (Specific Stack) and the Requirement for Full Amount
Gemara's Resolution: Both Cases Are Maneh Stam; Action Clarifies Intent
Dinar Ra (Damaged Coin) and the Distinction Between Defective and Worn Currency

macro_segments.title: Meshkon (Collateral) and the Distinction Between Giving Money and Giving Collateral

micro_segments.title: Rav Nachman: Meshkon Is Not Kessef Kiddushin
Rava's Challenge: The Braitta on Kedisha B'Meshkon
Rav Yitzchak's Source: Creditor Owns Collateral (from Parashat Mishpatim)
The Case of Rav Huna Bar Avin's Children: Priti (Money) Without Actual Payment

macro_segments.title: Rejection and Acceptance: The Woman's Response to the Money of Kiddushin

micro_segments.title: Destroying the Money: Throwing into Ocean or Fire as Implicit Rejection
Directing the Money to Others: Giving to Father and the Concept of Sheliach
The Case of Ploni: Random Third Party vs. Family Members
Placing on a Rock: Possession and Kinyan Chatzer
Feeding to a Dog: Benefit and Indirect Possession
